Brainstorming Market
Options
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 20 --

Approach #1 - Focus on YOU
▪ 	What are your core passions and interests?
▪ 	What skills have you developed?
▪ 	What knowledge have you acquired?
▪ 	What challenges have you overcome?
▪ 	What would other people say you do really well? What do
people ask you for advice about?
BONUS - Niche Selection
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 20 --

Approach #2 - Focus on YOUR AVATAR
▪ 	What do people to come to you for help with or ask you
questions about?
▪ 	What do your existing clients or prospects struggle with?
▪ 	What pain points, challenges, or common questions are
people bringing up in comments on blogs, other
programs, best-selling books on your topic, etc.
▪ 	If you have a list, ask them what they’re struggling with…
BONUS - Niche Selection
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 20 --

Approach #3 - FOLLOW THE MONEY
▪ 	Find out who is making money and see how you can
make/do it better
▪ 	Listen and hear what works – then improve upon it
▪ 	Evergreen markets (vs. seasonal or fad)
BONUS - Niche Selection
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 20 --

Approach #3 - FOLLOW THE MONEY
▪ 	Look for markets with multiple types of products at all
price points
▪ 	Go to Clickbank.com and search the marketplace by niche
topics
▪ 	Find a product on clickbank.com that is doing really
well in a niche that hasn’t been exposed to product
launches and focus there
▪ 	Focus in the “Big 4” proven markets and combine them or
niche down
BONUS - Niche Selection
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 20 --

Research and Test
▪ 	Google your niche and see what sites come up

-- 9 of 20 --

Research and Test
▪ 	Look for advertisers
▪ 	Test the Market
▪ 	Are people selling in these markets?
▪ 	If there is NO competition, then be very careful
▪ 	Find a successful market and be better/unique
▪ 	Find a market that people are passionate about
▪ 	Find rabid markets
BONUS - Niche Selection
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 20 --

Research and Test
▪ 	Look at popular magazines to get ideas
▪ 	Go to amazon.com and search for hot markets
▪ 	Is there a trade association for that market
▪ 	Is there a high “Cost per click” (CPC – $4-5 per click is
awesome)
▪ 	Join an affiliate program and observe
BONUS - Niche Selection
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 20 --

Research and Test
▪ 	Create a list of possible offers you could make and
analyze in terms of…
▪ 	Demand
▪ 	Market quality
▪ 	Speed to market
BONUS - Niche Selection
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 20 --

Research and Test
▪ 	Main factors for demand:
▪ 	Search volume (at least 100k searches for all main
keywords)
▪ 	Adwords – are people paying for advertisements
▪ 	Is there any competition?
▪ 	Are the prospects proven online buyers?
BONUS - Niche Selection
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 20 --

Research and Test
▪ 	Write the sales letter, even better, a squeeze page before
the product even exists and send traffic to it
▪ 	PPC traffic is quickest
▪ 	Look for good conversions (-20%)
▪ 	Once you’ve found your idea/niche, start building your
list and building a relationship with your list
BONUS - Niche Selection
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 20 --

What to Avoid…
▪ 	Thinking…I am not enough of an expert
▪ 	You don’t have to be the expert
▪ 	Leverage the knowledge of those who are experts
▪ 	You only have to be a few steps ahead to pull them
forward
▪ 	In the land of the blind, the one-eyed man is king!
▪ 	On the flip side, you don’t want to do anything or teach
anything that would put people in harm because you’re
“faking it”
BONUS - Niche Selection
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 15 of 20 --

What to Avoid…
▪ 	Can you help them…
▪ 	Take action?
▪ 	Stay accountable?
▪ 	Produce real results?
▪ 	(This is more important than your status as an expert)
BONUS - Niche Selection
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 20 --

What to Avoid…
▪ 	Following a passion without testing the market
▪ 	Offering what you think the market wants and not
what they actually want
▪ 	Not doing your research
▪ 	Not getting feedback in the development of your
product or service
BONUS - Niche Selection
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 17 of 20 --

What to Avoid…
▪ 	Not niching down enough…
▪ 	Trying to go broad and wide rather than narrow and
deep with your topic
▪ 	Trying to serve everyone
BONUS - Niche Selection
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
